label: json-viewer
info:
Example and testing of the uibuilder
`<json-viewer>` web component.

This component shows a JavaScript Object or
JSON string syntax-highlighted and searchable
as HTML.

There is also an exposed server-side rendering
export which outputs the HTML.

See [designs/json-viewer](uibuilder/docs/#/designs/json-viewer)
for details.

name: Rendered in Node-RED. The output is not reactive except for collapse/expand.
Just sending some data. This gets rendered by the client browser

RUN ONCE: Initialise Front-end Code - Run after the uibuilder node has been deployed. 
 REMEMBER to change the uib node name before use 
 Sets up FE code, reloads connected clients.
Convert input JSON to HTML

Example of using UIBUILDER's built-in <json-viewer> web component \n The component is most commonly used in the front-end where you load it as an optional library. \n However, as shown, it can also be used in Node.js and hence in Node-RED
This method is fully reactive. Search, etc all works
index.html
template:
JSON Viewer

Using UIBUILDER for Node-RED

Intro

Uses the <json-viewer> custom web component provided by uibuilder. The input data is sent to the browser from Node-RED.

Viewer examples

Example 1 - Complex JS Object, collapsed

Example 2 - Complex JS Object, Editable

Example 3 - Complex JS Object, send from Node-RED

name: index.js
function 8
func:
const defaultData = {
    standard: {
        string: "R1C1",
        "odd-prop-name": 42,
        "i-am": true,
        "date": new Date(),
        "array": [1, "a", 2, true, 3],
    },
    functions: {
        myCustomFn: function aDifferentName() { return true },
        arrowFn: () => { return true },
        asyncFn: async function aDifferentName2() { return true },
        asyncArrowFn: async () => { return true },
        generatorFn: function* mygeny() { yield 'Hello' },
        asyncGgeneratorFn: async function* mygeny2() { yield 'Goodby' },
    },
    specials: {
        "null": null,
        "undefined": undefined,
        "NaN": NaN,
        "Infinity": Infinity,
        "bigint": 9007199254740992n, // Larger than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER
        "symbol": Symbol("mySymbol"),
        // @ts-ignore
        "map": new Map([['key', 'value'], ['key-2', 42]]),
        "set": new Set([1, 2, 3]),
        "weakmap": new WeakMap([[{ id: 2 }, 'metadata']]),
        "weakset": new WeakSet([{ id: 1 }, { id: 2 }]),
        "regex": /test/gi,
        "error": new Error('Process failed'),
        "arrayBuffer": new ArrayBuffer(8),
        "uint8array": new Uint8Array([10, 20, 30]), // typed array
        "url": new URL('https://example.come/some/path?x=this,y=that'),
        "promise": new Promise(() => { }),
    },
    // Circular reference example
    circular: {}
}
defaultData.circular.self = defaultData
msg.payload = RED.util.uib.saferSerialize(defaultData)
return msg
